violations.text: Hot sauce was observed in the establishment from an unapproved source. Food unable to be proven from a licensed facility. Embargo released for food product to be voluntarily disposed of by person in charge. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025
Cans of food were observed with an accumulation of rodent droppings. Food voluntarily disposed of by person in charge. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

Frozen chocolate bananas (with other ingredients) packaged in the food establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the hood ventilation system has an accumulation of dust and grease. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

The following was observed: 1) The condensate from the ice machine is draining into a container. 2) The condensate from the air-conditioner is draining into the food prep sink. Condensate drainage and other non-sewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 02/24/2025

Hot sauce was observed in the establishment from an unapproved source. Item embargoed at the time of visit until source can be properly identified by a licensed facility/entity. Foods to remain embargoed until notified by RIDOH CFP. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025

Raw animal foods (such as chorizo and chicken) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as tomatoes and garlic) in the white 2 door refrigerator and stainless steel reach in cooler. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2025

Various items throughout the establishment was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2025

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chorizo was placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Foods moved to shallow pans to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2025

Frozen bananas packaged in mini freezer in establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

The presence of rodent activity is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings in kitchen, dry storage area, and gnaw marks/shaving of sprayed foam under the bakery area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment to continue USA pest control service visits 1x a week until rodent activity is resolved. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Dead roaches were observed on sticky glue traps in the establishment. Dead or trapped birds, insects, rodents, and other pests shall be removed from control devices and the PREMISES at a frequency that prevents their accumulation, decomposition, or the attraction of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 02/17/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the following (hood ventilation over stove and ice machine) have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Hot sauce was observed in the establishment from an unapproved source. Item embargoed at the time of visit until source can be properly identified by a licensed facility/entity. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2025
Observed establishment lacking allergy statement. Allergy statement posted at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025
Observed 6LB can pineapples with rodent droppings on top. PIC voluntarily disposed of the can during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Raw animal foods (such as chicken and beef) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (such as vegetables) in the walk-in refrigerator. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. PIC relocated food to proper area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Chorizo was observed at (50-64) degrees Fahrenheit held out of refrigeration next to cooking equipment. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge food was taken out of refrigeration under an hour prior to inspection. Food was put back into refrigeration to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Various items throughout the establishment was observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Employees' medicines were not stored to prevent the contamination of food, equipment, or utensils. PIC relocated medicines to designated areas. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Paint thinner was stored with food, equipment, utensils, or single service items. Chemicals must be stored to prevent the contamination of clean equipment, utensils linens, and/or single service by spacing or partitioning and locating them in an area that is not above food, equipment utensils, linens, and single service articles. PIC relocated painter thinner to designated area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Chlorine sanitizer solution was observed in excess of 200 parts per million (ppm). A chlorine sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be between 50-100 ppm. Proper sanitizing procedures reviewed with PIC at time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Frozen bananas packaged in mini freezer in establishment are lacking labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

The presence of rodent activity is not controlled as evidenced by rodent droppings in kitchen and front end of bakery. In addition, observed a live roach during time of this inspection. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

Following items were observed improperly stored:1. small plastic container used as a scoop in containers of food2. bowl used to scoop dry beansUtensils relocated to proper area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the following (speed racks, hood ventilation over stove and ice machine) have an accumulation of dust, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025

The following was observed: 1) The condensate from the ice machine is draining into a container. 2) The condensate line from the air conditioner was observed to be draining into the food prep sink. Condensate drainage and other nonsewage liquids must drain into a drain. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025
A hole was observed next to the hand washing station. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 02/05/2025
